topic: The work permit that expired because no one was tracking the renewal date
format: Before/after: describe exactly what this problem looks like unsolved versus what changes when it is solved
post:
The expiry date was already in the spreadsheet six months before it became a crisis. Everyone missed it anyway.

One renewal date slips. The employee gets flagged. Work stops. Now you're on the phone explaining to a client why their project is delayed — not because of something that broke today, but because of something that sat unnoticed for half a year.

This is what happens when work permit tracking lives in a spreadsheet nobody opens.

The date was right there. Column G. Quietly waiting to become someone's problem.

I've spoken with HR managers in Oman handling 50, 80, 100+ employees this way. Not because they're careless — because nothing is telling them when to act. WhatsApp threads don't send reminders. Excel doesn't escalate. It holds data and waits.

When tracking is automated, the reminder goes out 90 days before expiry. Then 60. Then 30. The right person gets notified. The renewal gets filed. Nothing stops.

That's the real gap. Not effort. Not intention. Just a system that actually follows through.

SmartPRO Hub tracks permits, visas, and Ministry of Labour Oman compliance deadlines in one place, with automated alerts before anything expires.
